title: For Myself Alone, I Would Not Be
content:
"For myself alone, I would not be
        Ambitious in my wish; but, for you,
        I would be trebled twenty times myself;
        A thousand times more fair,
        Ten thousand times more rich."
meaning:
Louisa May Alcott's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "For Myself Alone, I Would Not Be"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
